tier_1.title: Ensure AppArmor is installed and active
tier_1.vulnerability: Without AppArmor, all processes run with unrestricted OS permissions.

tier_2.title: Ensure ASLR is enabled (kernel.randomize_va_space = 2)
tier_2.vulnerability: Without ASLR, buffer overflow exploits use deterministic memory addresses.

tier_2.title: Ensure SUID core dumps are disabled (fs.suid_dumpable = 0)
tier_2.message: Live value: fs.suid_dumpable = '2' (expected '0'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: SUID core dumps can leak credentials and private keys from privileged process memory.

tier_2.title: Ensure core dump hard limit is 0
tier_2.message: '* hard core 0' not found in /etc/security/limits.conf or limits.d/
tier_2.vulnerability: Unlimited core dumps are a side-channel for extracting secrets.

tier_2.title: Ensure IP forwarding is disabled
tier_2.message: Live value: net.ipv4.ip_forward = '1' (expected '0'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: A forwarding laptop acts as a router, creating unintended network transit paths.

tier_2.title: Ensure send_redirects (all) is disabled
tier_2.message: Live value: net.ipv4.conf.all.send_redirects = '1' (expected '0'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: Sending ICMP redirects can misdirect other hosts' routing — MITM setup step.

tier_2.title: Ensure send_redirects (default) is disabled
tier_2.message: Live value: net.ipv4.conf.default.send_redirects = '1' (expected '0'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: Sending ICMP redirects can misdirect other hosts' routing — MITM setup step.

tier_2.title: Ensure accept_redirects (default) is disabled
tier_2.message: Live value: net.ipv4.conf.default.accept_redirects = '1' (expected '0'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: Accepting ICMP redirects lets an attacker silently reroute traffic.

tier_2.title: Ensure martian packet logging is enabled
tier_2.message: Live value: net.ipv4.conf.all.log_martians = '0' (expected '1'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: Martian packets reveal IP spoofing or severe network misconfiguration.

tier_2.title: Ensure IPv6 accept_redirects (all) is disabled
tier_2.message: Live value: net.ipv6.conf.all.accept_redirects = '1' (expected '0'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: IPv6 ICMP redirect attacks carry the same MITM risk as IPv4 but are often overlooked.

tier_2.title: Ensure IPv6 accept_redirects (default) is disabled
tier_2.message: Live value: net.ipv6.conf.default.accept_redirects = '1' (expected '0'); Not persisted in sysctl.d (found: None)
tier_2.vulnerability: IPv6 ICMP redirect attacks carry the same MITM risk as IPv4 but are often overlooked.

tier_2.title: Ensure IPv6 Router Advertisements are rejected
tier_2.message: accept_ra live: all=1, default=1; persisted: all=None, default=None. ⚠ Verify with 'ip -6 route' before applying if using IPv6 SLAAC.
tier_2.vulnerability: A rogue RA can silently replace the default IPv6 gateway and redirect all traffic.

tier_2.title: Ensure DCCP kernel module is blacklisted
tier_2.message: 'install dccp /bin/false' not found in modprobe.d; 'blacklist dccp' not found in modprobe.d
tier_2.vulnerability: DCCP has known kernel-level vulnerabilities and is never used in practice.

tier_2.title: Ensure TIPC kernel module is blacklisted
tier_2.message: 'install tipc /bin/false' not found in modprobe.d; 'blacklist tipc' not found in modprobe.d
tier_2.vulnerability: TIPC is a cluster protocol with no workstation use and known CVEs.

tier_2.title: Ensure SCTP kernel module is blacklisted
tier_2.message: 'install sctp /bin/false' not found in modprobe.d; 'blacklist sctp' not found in modprobe.d
tier_2.vulnerability: SCTP is rarely needed on workstations and has privilege escalation CVEs.
